page: Amazon Web Services r8in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized, network-optimized, and EBS-optimized eighth-generation server instance. Powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors, this instance features 96 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped across 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, providing high single-threaded and multi-threaded compute capacity. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware to maximize resource availability for guest workloads.

Designed for memory-intensive applications, the r8in.24xlarge provides 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This high resource density is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer and low-latency communication for distributed environments. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or hardware GPU accelerators, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes, which are optimized for high throughput on this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8in.24xlarge offers a highly specialized hardware profile that balances massive memory capacity with high-speed network performance. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for workloads that require substantial RAM and network throughput without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. The primary tradeoffs include the reliance on network-attached storage and the lack of local SSDs. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and real-time data analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.24xlarge is an eighth-generation, memory-optimized instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz, it delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The instance features 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it ensures minimal virtualization overhead and dedicated resource allocation. Network performance is a key highlight, offering a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. This hardware profile is highly optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time data analytics, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized, Nitro-based server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8in.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs, seven hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of RAM, and high-speed baseline network connectivity.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz
768.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core ratio
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture with dedicated resource allocation
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and real-time analytics
